title: cylindropuntia tesajo
intro: Cylindropuntia tesajo, also known as the tree cholla or cholla tree, is a species of cactus native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a type of columnar cactus with sharp, segmented stems that have been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Cylindropuntia tesajo is often made into a tea or infused in oil to be used topically, and its health benefits may include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting immune function.
